title: Class-AB BJT Audio Amplifier
connections: Input Signal → 10µF Coupling Cap → BC547 Base
BC547 Collector → Biasing Diodes (1N4148 ×2) → TIP41C Base (NPN) & TIP42C Base (PNP)
TIP41C Collector → VCC, TIP42C Collector → -VCC (or GND for single supply)
TIP41C & TIP42C Emitters → 0.47Ω Re Resistors → Output Node → 1000µF AC Cap → 8Ω Speaker
code:
Circuit Assembly & Tuning:
1. DC Bias: Adjust the collector current of the pre-amplifier driver stage to set the output node voltage to half of VCC.
2. Crossover Distortion: Use two biasing diodes (1N4148) to turn on the push-pull transistors slightly, preventing crossover distortion.
3. Coupling Capacitors: Use a large output capacitor (1000µF) to block DC current while passing AC audio signals down to 20Hz.
4. Thermal Stability: Place emitter degeneration resistors (0.47Ω) to prevent thermal runaway.
tips: Biasing diodes must match the thermal coefficient of push-pull transistors; mounting them close to output transistors prevents thermal runaway.
Always verify quiescent current consumption during startup. High current indicates transistor bias is set too high, risking burn-out.

title: Variable Regulated Power Supply
connections: Transformer Secondary → Bridge Rectifier (1N4007 ×4)
Bridge Rectifier Output (+) → 2200µF Filter Cap (+) → LM317 IN
LM317 OUT → 240Ω Resistor → LM317 ADJ Pin
LM317 ADJ Pin → 5kΩ Potentiometer → Ground Plane
LM317 OUT → 10µF Tantalum Output Cap (+) → Vout Terminal
code:
Assembly & Voltage Calibration:
1. Rectification: Assemble the diode bridge and filter capacitor to convert AC to DC.
2. Output Voltage Formula: Vout = 1.25V × (1 + Rpot / 240Ω).
3. Protection Diodes: Place a reverse diode from OUT to IN to protect the regulator from capacitor back-discharge during power-down.
4. Potentiometer: Use a linear taper potentiometer (type B) to ensure smooth voltage adjustments.
tips: The LM317 ADJ pin regulates by keeping a constant 1.25V reference between the OUT and ADJ pins.
Use tantalum capacitors on the output to improve transient response and reduce high-frequency ripple.

title: Li-Ion Constant-Current Charger
connections: DC Input (+) → LM317 IN
LM317 OUT → 0.22Ω current sense resistor → Li-Ion Battery (+)
BC547 Base/Emitter connected across 0.22Ω resistor
BC547 Collector → LM317 ADJ pin
code:
Calibration & Operations:
1. Constant Current Stage: When charging current exceeds 3A, the voltage drop across the 0.22Ω resistor exceeds 0.6V, turning on the BC547 transistor and pulling down ADJ to regulate current.
2. Voltage Limit: Adjust output voltage to a maximum of 4.2V using the regulator voltage divider.
3. Indication LED: Place an LED in series with the collector line to indicate active current limiting.
tips: Li-Ion batteries are highly sensitive. Never exceed 4.20V per cell; overcharging causes chemical instability.
For commercial applications, use a dedicated battery management IC (like the TP4056) for temperature and thermal cutoff protections.

title: 555 Astable Square Wave Oscillator
connections: NE555 Pin 1 → GND, Pin 8 → VCC, Pin 4 → VCC (Reset disabled)
NE555 Pin 3 → 220Ω Resistor → LED → GND
NE555 Pin 5 → 10nF Cap → GND
NE555 Pin 2 & 6 shorted → 10µF Cap (+) → GND
VCC → 1kΩ Resistor → Pin 7 → 10kΩ Potentiometer → Pin 6
code:
Tuning & Waveform Steps:
1. Timing Formula: Frequency = 1.44 / ((R1 + 2*R2) * C1).
2. Duty Cycle: Adjust the potentiometer to shift the output high/low duty ratio.
3. Noise Decoupling: Always bypass Control Voltage (Pin 5) with a 10nF ceramic capacitor to GND to prevent noise from triggering false clock states.
tips: The 555 output can source or sink up to 200mA, allowing it to drive buzzers or small relays directly without a transistor.
Modify output to a 50% duty cycle by placing a signal diode (1N4148) in parallel with R2.

title: Op-Amp Function Generator
connections: Op-Amp 1: Configured as a Schmitt trigger square-wave generator
Op-Amp 2: Configured as an integrator converting Square to Triangle waves
Op-Amp 3: Configured as an active waveshaper (diodes feedback) converting Triangle to Sine waves
VCC/GND connections to op-amp rail pins
code:
Tuning & Phase Alignment:
1. Square Wave Stage: Connect feedback resistors from output to non-inverting input of Op-Amp 1.
2. Integrator Stage: Connect output of Op-Amp 1 through a 10kΩ resistor to the inverting input of Op-Amp 2, with a 100nF capacitor in the feedback loop.
3. Frequency Control: Use a dual-gang potentiometer to adjust the integration resistance, shifting frequency output synchronously.
tips: Op-amp analog function generators require symmetrical dual power rails (e.g. ±12V) to output waveforms cleanly above and below ground.
Use high-speed, high slew-rate op-amps (like TL074) to prevent triangle wave peaks from rounding off at frequencies above 10kHz.
